fault_hypothesis: Line 72 uses a strict greater-than comparison (`now > cached_at + validated_ttl`) instead of greater-than-or-equal (`now >= cached_at + validated_ttl`), so an entry at exactly the TTL boundary is incorrectly treated as still valid rather than expired.

spec.expr: not (now == cached_at__1 + validated_ttl__1) or (now >= cached_at__1 + validated_ttl__1)

constraints.intent: At the TTL boundary (now == cached_at + ttl), the condition must treat the entry as expired; the strict > comparison fails this check.
After the expiry branch is not taken, now must be strictly less than the expiry boundary; equality should have triggered expiry.
Capture the boundary condition: when now equals cached_at + validated_ttl the entry should be expired, so the comparison operator must include equality.
Validated TTL must be positive so that the expiry boundary is meaningful.
